questions.question: Briefly explain why key management is considered as important as the encryption algorithm itself.
expectedPoints: The strength of encryption is dependent on the secrecy of the key.
If an encryption key is compromised, all data encrypted with that key is effectively breached.
Proper management (generation, storage, rotation, access control) ensures keys remain secure throughout their lifecycle.
Services like KMS and HSM are crucial for secure key management in the cloud.

questions.question: Compare and contrast symmetric and asymmetric encryption. In your answer, detail how they are used together in the TLS protocol to secure data in transit.
expectedPoints: Symmetric encryption uses one key, is fast, and is used for bulk data encryption. Asymmetric uses a key pair, is slower, and solves key distribution.
TLS uses asymmetric encryption first: server authentication (digital certificate) and secure exchange of a symmetric 'session key'.
The symmetric session key is then used to encrypt the actual data stream for the remainder of the session.
This combination provides the security benefits of asymmetric cryptography with the performance efficiency of symmetric cryptography.
